title: Making the Verb Match a Singular Subject

steps.explanation: We need to pick the verb form that matches the subject.

steps.instruction: Ask: Who or what does the action?
steps.explanation: The subject is 'The girl.' Since it is one person, it is singular.

steps.instruction: Singular subjects use the verb with -s. Plural subjects use the verb without -s.
steps.explanation: One girl = singular, so we use 'runs' (with -s). If it were 'The girls,' we would say 'The girls run fast' (no -s on the verb).

title: Matching a Plural Subject with the Right Verb

steps.explanation: We need to make the verb agree with the subject.

steps.subgoalLabel: Find the subject and its number
steps.instruction: Who does the action? Is it singular (one) or plural (more than one)?
steps.work: Subject = The birds. 'Birds' has an -s, so it is plural (many birds).
steps.explanation: The subject is plural because it refers to more than one bird.

steps.instruction: Plural subjects take the base verb (no -s). Singular subjects add -s to the verb.
steps.explanation: Plural subject 'birds' goes with 'sing' (no -s). It sounds odd to say 'The birds sings' — your ear can often help you check!
